_note: Camera block is IDENTICAL to site1_01_before_day.json — swap to edit_image and pass the before photo once it lands.

scene.description: A photorealistic wide daytime exterior of a 3,500 square foot irregular polygon residential common-space entry area in Lafayette Colorado suburban Front Range, Boulder County, newly installed as a low-water Front Range xeriscape hardscape. The entire 3500 sqft irregular polygon footprint is covered in tan 3/4 to 1-1/2 inch decomposed granite with a fully concealed black woven weed fabric underlayer. A series of warm-ochre weathered Colorado moss-rock boulders (18 to 36 inches) are placed in asymmetric odd-numbered clusters — groupings near the front-left, center-right, and an accent near the back wall. Four tons of buff Lyons flagstone accents sit flat on the rock as low stepping-stone groupings and one small seating stone cluster. Ten matte-black solar path lights line the implied walkway in a gentle curve. A second band of larger 2 to 6 inch rust-ochre rip-rap rock defines a dry-riverbed accent line running diagonally through the composition.
scene.weather: clear blue sky, high Front Range alpine haze with Flatirons visible in the distance
scene.location: Lafayette, Colorado, Boulder County (40.0004 N, -105.110 W)

materials.under_layer: black woven weed fabric, fully concealed — no edges visible

materials.type: weathered Colorado moss-rock boulders, ochre to deep red-brown with lichen dappling, 18 to 36 inches
materials.placement: three clusters of 3/2/1, odd-numbered, asymmetric, partially buried

materials.type: buff Lyons sandstone flagstone, irregular cleft-face slabs, 2-3 inch thick
materials.coverage: 4 tons across low stepping clusters and one seating grouping
materials.placement: informal walkable groupings, not a continuous path

materials.type: matte-black solar path lights, warm 2700K LED, 18 inch stake height
materials.placement: gentle curve along implied walkway, off (not glowing, it's midday)

camera.framing: eye-level wide shot, rule of thirds, back wall on upper third — IDENTICAL framing to site1_01_before_day

composition.foreground: decomposed granite field with one foreground boulder cluster
composition.midground: flagstone groupings, dry-riverbed accent line, additional boulders
composition.background: stucco wall and distant cottonwoods and scrub oak (unchanged from before)

style.reference: Sunset magazine landscape feature, Landscape Architecture Magazine project photo

include: accurate rock grain and color variation across granite field
realistic boulder weathering and partial burial
invisible weed fabric under the rock
avoid: floating rocks with no contact shadow
